goal: Generate production-ready place-specific set files with a binding 6x7 structure, where sets 1 and 2 are explicitly entertaining, story-led and place-near; set 4 returns to demanding concrete place logic; sets 3 and 5 are emne-driven; and set 6 is concept-driven.
core_principle: place -> set_mode -> source_pool -> stedskarakter -> spørsmålsvinkel -> konkret spørsmål; emner are background only in sets 1, 2 and 4, drivers only in sets 3, 5 and 6.
blocked_formulations: Hvorfor er dette stedet relevant for
Hva gjør stedet til et eksempel på
Hva gjør dette stedet relevant for
Hvordan fungerer stedet som eksempel på
Hvordan fungerer dette stedet som byrom
Hvorfor er dette et viktig grøntområde
Hva gjør dette stedet relevant for biologisk mangfold
Hvilket fagbegrep beskriver best dette stedet

gjenkjenning.purpose: Identify the place through a concrete signature trait.
good_openings: Hva ved stedet gjør det lett å kjenne igjen
Hva er det første som skiller
knowledge_requirements: must name a concrete place trait
must add why the trait matters in the city

historisk_endring.purpose: Frame the place through change over time.
knowledge_requirements: must mention at least one dated or periodized shift
must connect shift to current place character

teknisk_fysisk.purpose: Ask about construction, infrastructure, material or physical solution.
knowledge_requirements: must include a concrete technical or material detail
must explain what that detail enables

bruk.purpose: Ask about actual use, rhythms and user groups.
good_openings: Hva brukes stedet til i dag

romlig_lesning.purpose: Read movement, openness, closure, direction and position in space.
good_openings: Hva gjør at stedet åpner seg
knowledge_requirements: must tie it to movement or orientation

saertrekk.purpose: Distinguish the place from similar places in Oslo.
knowledge_requirements: must not fall back to generic category labels

kontrast.purpose: Use contrast against a nearby or comparable place.
good_openings: Hva er den tydeligste forskjellen mellom
Hvilket trekk gjør at dette ikke fungerer som
knowledge_requirements: must compare against an explicit contrast target or same-type place
must explain difference, not just name it

tidslag.purpose: Read multiple periods or surviving traces in the same place.
good_openings: Hvilke epoker kan fortsatt leses her
knowledge_requirements: must identify at least one surviving trace
must connect trace to present-day reading

begrepsdefinisjon.purpose: Define or identify a core concept that actually helps read the place.

begrepskontrast.purpose: Distinguish between two close concepts, terms or analytical pairs.

begrepsanvendelse.purpose: Apply a concept to the place instead of only defining it abstractly.

question_families.story: Question built around a memorable local story, turning point or anecdote tied to the place.
question_families.kuriositet: Question built around an unexpected, quirky or surprising detail that makes the place memorable.
question_families.person: Question anchored in a person strongly tied to the place through use, design, event, statue, institution or memory.
question_families.hendelse: Question anchored in an event, happening, controversy, opening, accident, performance or public moment at the place.
question_families.scene: Question anchored in a vivid place-situation the player can imagine or observe on site.
question_families.visuell_hook: Question anchored in something visually striking, bodily noticeable or easy to point at in the place.

set_specific_rules.1: Knowledge should feel like bonus knowledge: vivid, concise and memorable, with at least one extra detail beyond the answer.
set_specific_rules.2: Knowledge should connect people, events, use or curious details to how the place lives in the city.
set_specific_rules.3: Knowledge should anchor terminology in the place and not stay generic.
set_specific_rules.4: Knowledge should deepen the concrete place story through institutions, functions, people or development, while staying lively.
set_specific_rules.5: Knowledge should show analytical reading, not just terminology.
set_specific_rules.6: Knowledge should define the concept clearly and show why it helps interpret this place.

output_mapping.dimension: Derived from angle_rules.dimension_hints and/or emne dimensions.
output_mapping.topic: Concrete place topic in sets 1,2,4; faglig topic in sets 3,5,6.
output_mapping.emne_id: Optional support field in sets 1,2,4; required and load-bearing in sets 3,5,6.
output_mapping.related_emner: Fill from emne relations and mapping context, especially in sets 3,5,6.
output_mapping.core_concepts: Required in sets 3,5,6 when concept-bearing emner are used.
output_mapping.concept_focus: Required in set 6; optional but useful in sets 3 and 5.
output_mapping.tags: Use place_type, subtype, set mode and major thematic markers.
output_mapping.question_scope: place for sets 1,2,4; emne for sets 3,5; concept for set 6.
output_mapping.difficulty: Low in set 1, mixed in sets 2-5, conceptually hardest in set 6.
output_mapping.question_type: fact/use/analysis/comparison in sets 1-5; concept/definition/concept_application in set 6.
output_mapping.source: Must list the concrete sources, files or place/emne evidence used to draft the question.
output_mapping.targetId: For by place quizzes, copy placeId into targetId on every question and at file level.

park.extra_rule: Do not let two parks in the same batch share the same subtype+primary family combination.

knutepunkt.extra_rule: Force at least one question on flows, transfers or waiting.

gate.extra_rule: Prefer street character, commerce, rhythm or transformation over generic byrom wording.

boligomrade.extra_rule: At least one question should distinguish the area from another housing type in Oslo.

plass.extra_rule: At least one question should address the place as a scene, threshold or civic room.

omrade.extra_rule: Prefer large-scale area logic over single-building detail.

generator_pipeline: 1. Read place, place.quiz_profile, optional entertaining source fields, emne mappings, emnemapping and relevant prior quiz context.
2. Build a six-set plan using binding_set_structure and set_mode_rules.
3. For set 1: collect stories, curiosities, visual hooks, memorable local facts and must_include traits before consulting emner.
4. For set 2: collect related people, events, institutions, place rhythm, use, architecture and notable concrete details before consulting emner.
5. For sets 1, 2 and 4: concrete place-near source pools must be exhausted before emner can be used as support metadata.
6. For sets 3 and 5: choose mapped emner that best support the place quiz_profile primary_angles and contrast needs.
7. For set 6: derive concepts from core_concepts, concept_focus, emner, mapping and prior quiz language.
8. Generate candidate questions within each set mode and required family spread.
9. Reject blocked formulations and dry generic phrasings, especially in sets 1 and 2.
10. Write knowledge so it adds bonus information, context and place feel beyond the answer.
11. Validate per-set hard requirements, field contract and cross-set repetition limits.
12. Emit production-ready 6x7 set file and flat export if needed.
9. Stamp output fields per question: targetId=placeId, question_scope from set mode, and question_layer from the bound set-mode map.
10. Validate against the superset output contract: required fields, field order preference, targetId consistency and question_layer consistency.
validator_checks: File has exactly 6 sets.
Each set has exactly 7 questions.
set_id follows by_<place_id>_set_<1-6>.
Sets 1, 2 and 4 are place-driven and not emne-driven.
Sets 3 and 5 are emne-driven.
Set 6 is concept-driven.
No blocked formulation in question text.
Question text must mention or clearly hinge on a place-specific trait in all sets.
Knowledge must not be answer-only.
No more than one question per emne-angle pair per set unless set 6 explicitly compares concepts.
At least three distinct question families in each set except set 6, which may use concept families but must still vary its concept task types.
At least two signature_features or must_include items represented in each set.
If place has contrast_targets, at least one comparison question across the full place quiz.
Set 6 must use at least four distinct core concepts or concept pairs across the seven questions.
No question should be a thin rewording of another question in the same file.
Options must be plausible and answerIndex must match answer.
Manifest must point to each set individually.
Set 1 has at least 4 story/curiosity-driven questions.
Set 1 has at least 2 visual or sensory questions.
Set 1 has 0 definition questions.
Set 2 has at least 1 person question and 1 event question.
Set 2 has at least 1 question about rhythm/use and 1 about a noticed physical trait.
Sets 1 and 2 do not read like mini emnesett in disguise.
Knowledge in sets 1 and 2 contains bonus information and not just answer restatement.
Every question must include targetId and it must equal placeId for by place quizzes.
Every question must include question_layer.
question_layer must match the active set mode.
question_scope must match the active set mode and stay consistent with question_layer.

binding_set_structure.binding_rule: Only sets 3, 5 and 6 may be emne-/begrepsdriven. Set 1 must be an entertaining intro/story set. Set 2 must foreground place life, people, events and curiosities. Sets 1, 2 and 4 must be built from concrete place-specific information first, not from emne wording.

1.description: Entertaining opening set built from stories, curiosities, visual hooks and memorable concrete facts.

2.description: Concrete and lively set built from people, events, use, rhythm, architecture and notable details.

4.description: Return to the place with more demanding but still concrete logic: people, institutions, functions, development lines and specific traits.

5.description: Second faglig set with deeper structure and analysis.
must_prioritize: terminologi fra emner og tidligere quiz
begreper som faktisk hjelper spilleren å lese stedet

file_shape.targetId_rule: File-level targetId must equal the placeId / place target of the full quiz file.

question_shape.targetId_rule: For by place quizzes, targetId must equal placeId on every question. It is required even when the file-level targetId is present.
question_shape.question_layer_rule: Every question must carry a question_layer that matches the set mode. The generator may not leave this inferred or blank.

question_shape_notes.question_scope: Use place in sets 1,2,4; emne in sets 3,5; concept in set 6.
question_shape_notes.question_type: Prefer fact/story/person/event/scene in sets 1,2,4; analysis or concept types only from set 3 onward.
question_shape_notes.source: Early sets should cite concrete place-near source material when possible.
question_shape_notes.question_layer: Bind question_layer to set mode: set 1=intro_story, set 2=life_people_events, set 3=emne_foundation, set 4=place_advanced, set 5=emne_advanced, set 6=concept.
question_shape_notes.targetId: Repeat the place target on every question to match the superset quiz schema and simplify downstream filtering.

sets_1_2_4.rule: emne_id, related_emner, core_concepts and concept_focus may be present, but the question must originate from place-specific facts, traits, functions, institutions or concrete history.

sets_3_5_6.rule: emne_id, related_emner, core_concepts and concept_focus must be meaningfully populated and should actively structure the question.

authoring_workflow: Build set 1 from stories, quirks, visual hooks and memorable concrete place facts first.
Build set 2 from people, events, use, rhythm, architecture and notable details tied to the place.
Only then check emnekart, emnemapping and relevant emner for support metadata.
Build set 3 from the strongest mapped emner.
Build set 4 as a concrete advanced place set using institutions, people, functions and development lines.
Build set 5 from deeper emne structure and terminology.
Build set 6 from concepts, definitions and concept contrasts tied to the place.

optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.stories: Short local stories, anecdotes or turning points that can seed set 1 or 2 questions.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.fun_facts: Quick surprising facts or memorable details.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.related_people: Named people strongly tied to the place.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.related_events: Events, openings, protests, accidents, concerts or public happenings tied to the place.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.visual_hooks: Things players can immediately see, notice or imagine on site.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.quirks: Oddities, paradoxes, nicknames, slang or peculiar local traits.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.institutions: Institutions, venues, buildings or organizations that matter for the place story.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.artifacts: Statues, signs, infrastructure, artworks or objects that can anchor concrete questions.

place_profile_extensions.fallback_rule: If these fields are missing, the generator must derive equivalent material from desc, popupDesc, signature_features, must_include and mapped quiz context before falling back to emner.

source_pool_rules.rule: For sets 1, 2 and 4, the generator must exhaust concrete place-near source pools before drawing on emne wording or abstract analytical language.

entertainment_rules.surprise_value_definition: A question has surprise value if the answer or knowledge contains a memorable, unexpected or vivid detail likely to stick with the player.
entertainment_rules.bonus_info_definition: Knowledge should often contain one extra detail, contrast, anecdote or sensory cue beyond what is needed to justify the answer.

superset_sync.purpose: Align quiz generator output contract and validator with the canonical question superset while keeping the v3 place-first set logic.
key_changes: targetId added as required field on each question
question_layer added as required field on each question
field order aligned more closely with supersetQUIZMAL
validator checks added for targetId and question_layer consistency

field_binding_rules.targetId: Must be emitted on every question and must equal the place target for by place quizzes.
field_binding_rules.question_layer: Must be emitted on every question and must match the active set mode.
field_binding_rules.question_scope: Must match set mode and stay in sync with question_layer.
